paper_metadata.title: GODEL: Large-Scale Pre-Training for Goal-Directed Dialog
paper_metadata.abstract: We introduce GODEL (Grounded Open Dialogue Language Model), a large pre-trained language model for dialog. In contrast with earlier models such as DialoGPT, GODEL leverages a new phase of grounded pre-training designed to better support adapting GODEL to a wide range of downstream dialog tasks that require information external to the current conversation (e.g., a database or document) to produce good responses. Experiments against an array of benchmarks that encompass task-oriented dialog, conversational QA, and grounded open-domain dialog show that GODEL outperforms state-of-the-art pre-trained dialog models in few-shot fine-tuning setups, in terms of both human and automatic evaluation. A novel feature of our evaluation methodology is the introduction of a notion of utility that assesses the usefulness of responses (extrinsic evaluation) in addition to their communicative features (intrinsic evaluation). We show that extrinsic evaluation offers improved inter-annotator agreement and correlation with automated metrics. Code and data processing scripts are publicly available.

extraction_data.innovation: GODEL introduces a three-phase pre-training strategy that incorporates web text, dialog corpora, and grounded dialog datasets to improve utility in goal-directed dialog tasks. It leverages external information (e.g., retrieved documents) to generate contextually grounded responses, enabling better adaptation to downstream tasks requiring external knowledge.
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Grounded pre-training with web text, dialog corpora, and grounded dialog datasets
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Web documents (public web text), Reddit comment chains (DialoGPT), DSTC7 Task2, MS MARCO, UnifiedQA, Schema-Guided Dialog
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Few-shot fine-tuning on goal-directed dialog tasks (e.g., MultiWOZ, CoQA, Wizard of Wikipedia, Wizard of the Internet)
extraction_data.extension: Three-phase pre-training: (1) linguistic pre-training on web documents, (2) dialog pre-training on Reddit comment chains, and (3) grounded dialog pre-training on knowledge-grounded and task-oriented datasets.
extraction_data.research_problem: Goal-directed dialog generation with grounding in external knowledge or environment
extraction_data.application: Knowledge-grounded response generation, task-oriented dialog, conversational question answering, chat
